scope: Pinpointed sources used specifically to ground adversarial review records without silently changing the evidential direction of the main Chapter 6 graph.
governing_rules: A source in this ledger grounds a live objection or scope restriction; inclusion does not establish that the objection succeeds.
Source-grounded is weaker than survives-review: the latter additionally requires a discriminating test and a recorded result.
No entry may be used to infer that a current artificial system is conscious or nonconscious.
A partially verified entry must state exactly which bibliographic and proposition-level elements were checked.

relationship_types.contradictory: The source advances a claim incompatible with the reviewed proposition as stated.
relationship_types.undercutting: The source challenges the proposition's warrant without asserting its negation.
relationship_types.alternative_explanation: The source supplies a rival account capable of explaining the target evidence.
relationship_types.scope_restriction: The source shows that the proposition requires narrower conditions or cannot support its broadest reading.

sources.title: Explanatory models in neuroscience: Part 1—Taking mechanistic abstraction seriously
verified_elements: Title, authors, date, and arXiv identifier were checked against the official arXiv record.
The official abstract explicitly argues that some neural-network models can qualify as mechanistic models under a model-to-mechanism mapping and describes the proposed 3M++ criteria.
The abstract states that the relevant description must be abstract but sufficiently detailed to be runnable and mapped using principles continuous with experimental neuroscience.
unverified_elements: A line-by-line audit of the full manuscript has not yet been recorded in this repository.
The source does not establish consciousness, phenomenality, or mechanism preservation for any current AI system.
sources.supports_objection: Material dissimilarity from nervous tissue does not by itself disqualify an engineered model from mechanistic status; an abstract but intervention-relevant mapping may be scientifically legitimate.
does_not_establish: That behavioral equivalence alone proves mechanistic equivalence.
That any existing language model preserves a consciousness-theory mechanism.
That the proposed mapping criteria are sufficient for phenomenal consciousness.

edges.objection_scope: The proposition must reject vocabulary-only analogy without treating biological dissimilarity as sufficient evidence that an engineered implementation lacks the relevant mechanism.
edges.forced_concession_supported: A nonbiological model may count as mechanistically explanatory when a sufficiently detailed, runnable, intervention-relevant model-to-system mapping is demonstrated.
edges.does_not_support: That any present AI system satisfies such a mapping or that mechanistic similarity settles consciousness.

pinpoint_locator.value: Official arXiv record and abstract for arXiv:2104.01490, especially the abstract's statement of mechanistic mapping and 3M++ criteria.

edges.verification_note: The source directly grounds the scope restriction against substrate-based dismissal. Applying it to consciousness-theory mechanisms remains a bounded project inference and must be tested through the linked intervention protocol.
